content: Which of the following is a Fittig reaction?
explanation:
Step 1: Recall the reactions

  • Wurtz reaction: Coupling of two alkyl halides with sodium in dry ether → alkane.

    $ 2R–X + 2Na \xrightarrow[dry\ ether]{} R–R + 2NaX $

  • Wurtz–Fittig reaction: Coupling of an aryl halide and an alkyl halide with sodium in dry ether → alkyl-substituted aromatic compound (like toluene).

    $ Ar–X + R–X + 2Na \xrightarrow[dry\ ether]{} Ar–R + 2NaX $

  • Fittig reaction: Coupling of two aryl halides with sodium in dry ether → biaryl compound.

    $ 2Ar–X + 2Na \xrightarrow[dry\ ether]{} Ar–Ar + 2NaX $

Step 2: Check each option

  • Option A:

    $\text{C}_6\text{H}_5\text{Br} + \text{CH}_3\text{Br} + 2Na → \text{C}_6\text{H}_5CH₃$

    This is Wurtz–Fittig reaction.

  • Option B:

    $2C_2H_5Br + 2Na → C_2H_5–C_2H_5$

    This is Wurtz reaction (alkyl–alkyl coupling).

  • Option C:

    $2CH_3Br + 2Na → CH_3–CH_3$

    This is also Wurtz reaction.

  • Option D:

    $2C_6H_5Br + 2Na → C_6H_5–C_6H_5$

    This is Fittig reaction.

✅ Final Answer:

Option D is the Fittig reaction.

content: What is the difference in molar mass of Undecane and Dodecane?
explanation: Undecane $\mathrm{C}_{11} \mathrm{H}_{24}$ and dodecane $\mathrm{C}_{12} \mathrm{H}_{26}$ are members of homologous series of alkanes. Successive homologues differ by one $-\mathrm{CH}_2$ (methylene) unit which results in a molecular weight difference of 14 units between each successive compound.

content: What is the molar mass of third homologue of alkane series?
explanation:
Two successive homologues differ by one $-\mathrm{CH}_2-$ (methylene) unit, i.e. by a molar mass of $(12+2) \mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}=14 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$.

Molar mass of first member of alkane series $\left(\mathrm{CH}_4\right)=16 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$

$\therefore \quad$ Molar mass of third member

$$ =16+14+14=44 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1} $$

Two successive homologues differ by one $-\mathrm{CH}_2$ (methylene) unit i.e., molecular weight of each successive member differs by 14 units.

options.content: Alkanes with less than 10 C atoms
Alkanes having 20 C to 25 C atoms
Alkanes having 25 C to 30 C atoms
Alkanes having more than 35 C atoms

content: Which of the following compounds has highest boiling point?
explanation: In straight chain alkane molecules, surface area is relatively larger as compared to branched chain alkanes and as a result, the intermolecular forces are relatively stronger in straight chain alkanes than in branched chain alkanes. Hence, straight chain alkanes have higher melting and boiling points as compared to branched alkanes.

content: Identify the name of reaction for aryl halide with alkyl halide and sodium metal in dry ether to give substituted aromatic compounds.
explanation:
The correct answer is Option B: Wurtz-Fittig reaction.

Explanation:

  • When an aryl halide (like chlorobenzene) reacts with an alkyl halide (like bromoethane) in the presence of sodium metal and dry ether, a substituted aromatic compound is formed.

  • The general reaction is:

    $$ \text{Aryl halide} + \text{Alkyl halide} + 2\,\text{Na} \xrightarrow{\text{dry ether}} \text{Alkyl arene} + 2\,\text{NaX} $$

  • This reaction is called the Wurtz-Fittig reaction.

Other options:

  • Wurtz reaction (Option A): Involves two alkyl halides, not aryl halide and alkyl halide.

  • Fittig reaction (Option C): Involves only aryl halides; produces biaryl compounds.

  • Finkelstein reaction (Option D): Is a halogen exchange reaction, not relevant here.

Therefore, the answer is Option B.

Which of the following is one of the product of ozonolysis?
explanation:
Ozonolysis is an organic reaction where ozone $(O_3)$ cleaves double or triple bonds (mainly in alkenes), resulting in the formation of two carbonyl compounds.

For a simple alkene, ozonolysis generally gives aldehydes and/or ketones as products. The type depends on the substituents present at the double bond.

In the given options:

  • Alcohol is not a direct product.

  • Acid may form as a further oxidation product but not from simple ozonolysis.

  • Aldehyde is a primary product.

  • Ester is not a product of ozonolysis.

Answer:

Option C: Aldehyde

content: What is molecular formula of undecane?
explanation:
The molecular formula of undecane is:

$$ \mathrm{C}_{11} \mathrm{H}_{24} $$

Undecane belongs to the alkane family, which follows the general formula:

$$ \mathrm{C}_n \mathrm{H}_{2n+2} $$

For undecane, where $ n = 11 $:

$$ \mathrm{H} = 2(11) + 2 = 24 $$

Hence, the molecular formula is $ \mathrm{C}_{11} \mathrm{H}_{24} $, which corresponds to Option A.

content: What is the product of Hydroborationoxidation of but-1-ene?
explanation:
The hydroboration-oxidation of an alkene involves two steps. The first step, hydroboration, adds borane ($BH_3$) to the alkene in a syn-addition manner, forming an organoborane intermediate. The second step, oxidation, treats this intermediate with hydrogen peroxide ($H_2O_2$) in the presence of a base (usually sodium hydroxide, $NaOH$), replacing the boron atom with a hydroxyl group.

For but-1-ene, the hydroboration-oxidation follows these steps:

Hydroboration (anti-Markovnikov addition): The boron atom adds to the less substituted carbon (the terminal carbon) of the but-1-ene, leading to the formation of a trialkylborane intermediate.

Oxidation: The organoborane intermediate is oxidized, replacing the boron atom with a hydroxyl group, resulting in the formation of an alcohol.

For but-1-ene, the reaction specifically yields butan-1-ol as the product, because the hydroxyl group is added to the terminal carbon (originally bonded with boron) due to the anti-Markovnikov addition pattern.

Therefore, the product of hydroboration-oxidation of but-1-ene is:

Option C: Butan-1-ol

content: The correct stability order of alkyl substituted alkenes is
explanation:
Order of stability of alkyl substituted alkenes:

$$\mathrm{R}_2 \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CR}_2>\mathrm{R}_2 \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CHR}>\mathrm{R}_2 \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CH}_2$$

Greater is the number of alkyl groups attached to the doubly bonded carbon atoms, greater is the stability of alkene.

content: Which among the following is NOT a feature of $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$ mechanism?

options.content: Involves simultaneous bond breaking and bond forming.

explanation:
Answer: Option C (Formation of planar carbocation intermediate)

Explanation:

The SN2 mechanism is characterized by:

A single-step process where the nucleophile attacks the electrophilic carbon from the side opposite to the leaving group (backside attack).

Simultaneous bond formation and bond breaking: As the nucleophile bonds to the carbon, the leaving group leaves in a concerted manner.

No carbocation intermediate is formed. Instead, the transition state involves both the nucleophile and the leaving group partially bonded to the central carbon.

In contrast, SN1 mechanisms involve the formation of a planar carbocation intermediate after the leaving group departs.

Thus, the statement "Formation of planar carbocation intermediate" does not apply to the SN2 mechanism.

content: What is the numerical difference in molar masses of second and third member of a homologous series?
explanation:
In a homologous series, each successive member differs by a CH$_{2}$ group, which consists of one carbon atom and two hydrogen atoms. The molar mass of a carbon atom is approximately 12 g/mol and that of a hydrogen atom is approximately 1 g/mol. Therefore, the molar mass of a CH$_{2}$ group is:

$$ \text{Molar mass of CH}_2 = 12 \, \text{g/mol (for C)} + 2 \times 1 \, \text{g/mol (for 2 H)} = 14 \, \text{g/mol} $$

Thus, the numerical difference in molar masses between any two successive members of a homologous series is 14 g/mol. Therefore, the numerical difference in molar masses of the second and third member of a homologous series is 14 g/mol.

Option A: 14

Identify the correct stability order of following alkenes.

I) $\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_2 \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{C}\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_2$

II) $\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_2 \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CH}_2$

III) $\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_2 \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CHCH}_3$
explanation:
The alkene that has greater number of alkyl groups attached to doubly bonded carbon atoms is more stable than other alkenes.

$\therefore$ Order of stability of alkenes with respect to Saytzeff rule is :

$$\begin{aligned} & \text { Saytzeff rule is : } \\\\ & \left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_2 \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{C}\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_2>\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_2 \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CH}\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)> \\\\ & \left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_2 \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CH}_2 \end{aligned}$$

Which from following has highest boiling point?
explanation:
Boiling points in hydrocarbons generally increase with the number of carbon atoms present in the molecule. This is because more carbon atoms typically mean a larger molecular size and greater van der Waals forces, which require more energy (in the form of heat) to overcome. Additionally, branching in a molecule reduces its boiling point because it decreases the surface area, leading to weaker van der Waals forces.

In the provided options, n-pentane has the highest boiling point. It consists of a straight chain of five carbon atoms, which maximizes the surface area and resulting van der Waals forces compared to its branched or shorter-chain counterparts.

Identify the product formed by the action of $$\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})$$ with $$\mathrm{CO}(\mathrm{g})$$ in presence of $$\mathrm{Ni}$$ ?
explanation:
The reaction of $$\mathrm{H}_2(g)$$ with $$\mathrm{CO}(g)$$ in presence of $$\mathrm{Ni}$$ is as follows :

$$\underset{\text { Hydrogen }}{3 \mathrm{H}_2(g)}+\underset{\substack{\text { Carbon } \\ \text { monoxide }}}{\mathrm{CO}(g)} \xrightarrow[\text { Methane }]{\mathrm{Ni}} \mathrm{CH}_4(g)+\underset{\text { Water }}{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}}(g)$$

Thus, products formed are methane and water.

content: What is the number of moles of nascent hydrogen required to prepare 1 mole of methane from iodomethane?
explanation:
$$\mathrm{\mathop {C{H_3} - I}\limits_{Methyl\,iodide} + \mathop {2[H]}\limits_{Nascent} \buildrel {Zn,\,HCl} \over \longrightarrow \mathop {C{H_4}}\limits_{Methane} + HI}$$

Hence, the number of moles of nascent hydrogen required to prepare 1 mole of methane from iodomethane is 2 moles.

content:
Identify the product obtained in following reaction.

n $$\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{MgI}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \stackrel{\text { dry ether }}{\longrightarrow}$$ product
explanation:
This is the reaction of methylmagnesium iodide (CH₃MgI), a Grignard reagent, with water (H₂O). The general reaction of a Grignard reagent with water is:

$ \mathrm{R-MgX} + \mathrm{H_2O} \rightarrow \mathrm{R-H} + \mathrm{MgX(OH)} $

where R-MgX is the Grignard reagent (in this case, CH₃MgI, where R = CH₃ and X = I) and R-H is the corresponding hydrocarbon (in this case, CH₄).

So, when CH₃MgI reacts with water, the product will be methane (CH₄) and magnesium hydroxide iodide (MgI(OH)).

Therefore, the correct option is :

Option D : n CH₄ and n MgI(OH).

1 molecule of isopentane has 1 secondary carbon atom.

$$\therefore \quad 1 \mathrm{~mol}$$ isopentane $$=1 \mathrm{~mol}$$ secondary carbon atoms

$$\therefore \quad \mathrm{n}$$ mol isopentane $$=\mathrm{n}$$ mol secondary carbon atoms

content: Which among the following alkenes is most stable?
explanation:
Order of stability of alkyl substituted alkenes:

$$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{R}_2 \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CR}_2>\mathrm{R}_2 \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CHR}>\mathrm{R}_2 \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CH}_2, \\ & \mathrm{RCH}=\mathrm{CHR}>\mathrm{RCH}=\mathrm{CH}_2>\mathrm{CH}_2=\mathrm{CH}_2 . \end{aligned}$$

Greater is the number of alkyl groups attached to the doubly bonded carbon atoms, greater is the stability of alkene.

content: Which of the following compounds has highest boiling point?

content: Which of the following is general representation of Grignard reagent?
explanation:
Grignard reagents are a class of organomagnesium compounds typically represented by the general formula $ \mathrm{R}-\mathrm{Mg}-\mathrm{X} $, where R is an organic group (like an alkyl or aryl group) and X is a halogen (such as chlorine, bromine, or iodine). Therefore, the correct option for the general representation of a Grignard reagent is :

Option C : $ \mathrm{R}-\mathrm{Mg}-\mathrm{X} $

content: What is the range of number of carbon atoms in alkanes found in paraffin wax?
explanation: Carbon twenty $\left(\mathrm{C}_{20}\right)$ to carbon thirty $\left(\mathrm{C}_{30}\right)$ range of carbon atoms in alkanes found in paraffin wax. It is a soft colourless solid derived from petroleum, coal or shale oil that consists of a mixture of hydrocarbon molecules. It is used in candles, wax paper, polishes, cosmetics and electrical insulators.

content: Identify the hydrocarbon compound from following containing carbon atoms in the range of $$\mathrm{C}_6$$ to $$\mathrm{C}_8$$ ?
explanation: Petrol hydrocarbon compound containing carbon atom in the range of $$\mathrm{C}_6$$ to $$\mathrm{C}_8$$. It is a mixture of several different hydrocarbons. The most commonly found molecules are alkanes (linear or branched), cycloalkanes. Each petroleum variety has a unique mixture of molecules, which define its physical and chemical properties. The alkanes from pentane $$(\mathrm{C}_5 \mathrm{H}_{22})$$ to octane $$(\mathrm{C}_8 \mathrm{H}_{88})$$ are refined into petrol.

content: The alkane formed on heating sodium butanoate with sodalime is
explanation:
The alkane formed on heating sodium butanoate with sodalime is propane. In this reaction, the product contains one carbon atom less than the carboxylic acid. This process of elimination of carbon dioxide from a carboxylic acid is known as decarboxylation.

$$\mathrm{\mathop {C{H_3}C{H_2}C{H_2}CO{O^ - }N{a^ - }}\limits_{Sodium\,bu\tan oate} + NaOH\mathrel{\mathop{\kern0pt\longrightarrow} \limits_\Delta ^{CaO}} \mathop {C{H_3}C{H_2}C{H_3}}\limits_{\Pr opane} + N{a_2}C{O_3}}$$

explanation:

content: Propene when treated with cold conc. $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ forms a compound which on heating with water gives
explanation: Propene when treated with cold conc. $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ forms a compound, isopropyl-hydrogen sulphate on heating with water gives propan-2-ol.

content: How many gram of sodium (atomic mass $23u$) is required to prepare one mole of ethane from methyl chloride by Wurtz reaction?
explanation:
The Wurtz reaction between methyl chloride and sodium metal in presence of dry ether can be written as :

$$\mathrm{2C{H_3}Cl + 2Na\buildrel {Dry\,ether} \over \longrightarrow \mathop {C{H_3} - C{H_3}}\limits_{Ethane} + 2NaCl}$$

$\Rightarrow 2$ moles of sodium metal reacts to give 1 mole of ethane.

Also,

1 mole of Na metal $=23 \mathrm{~g}$

$\therefore 2$ moles of Na metal $=23 \times 2=46 \mathrm{~g}$

Thus, 46 g of sodium is required to prepare one mole of ethane from methyl chloride by Wurtz reaction.

explanation:
$2 n R-X \xrightarrow[\text { Dry ether }]{+2 n \mathrm{Na}} R \underset{\text { Alkane }}- R+2 \mathrm{NaX}$

The above given reaction is known as Wurtz reaction.
